Title: Cytochrome c oxidase inactivation in Physcomitrium patens reveals that respiration coordinates plant metabolism

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome c oxidase assembly protein 11; mitochondrial copper chaperone/assembly factor for Complex IV
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues; highest transcript levels in imbibed spores
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected broadly; KO lines lacked detectable transcript
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long day versus continuous light; elevated CO2; glucose and ammonia supplementation; amino acid supplementation; dark/night versus day sampling (ZT0, ZT2, ZT6)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein synthesis and growth; nitrogen assimilation and carbon-nitrogen metabolic balance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of COX11 abolishes cytochrome c oxidase (Complex IV) activity, severely impairs growth, alters carbon metabolism, reduces carbon fixation efficiency, lowers abundance of multiple amino acids, and disrupts carbon-nitrogen coordination
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout via homologous recombination; RT-PCR; RNA-seq; BN-PAGE in-gel CIV activity staining; spectrophotometric CIV assay; respiratory inhibitor assays; metabolomics; growth rescue by exogenous amino acids
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: COX1 (assembly target for CuB center insertion); respiratory Complex IV/cytochrome c oxidase
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1219350); untargeted metabolomics by GC-MS and IC-MS
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severely impaired growth; delayed development; prolonged protonema phase; failure to induce spore production under standard conditions; starch overaccumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: COX11 is not a pathway enzyme or TF; it is a respiratory complex assembly factor, therefore classified as Common Gene. cox11 mutants retained O2 consumption mainly through AOX-dependent cyanide-insensitive respiration. Growth was partially rescued by several amino acids, especially serine/glycine-related supplementation, but not by sugar supply.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a mechanistic target rather than a crop improvement target; highlights that maintaining mitochondrial cytochrome pathway function is essential for efficient amino acid biosynthesis and carbon-nitrogen coordination in photosynthetic tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COX11 knockout in Physcomitrium patens eliminated detectable cytochrome c oxidase/Complex IV activity. 2. Despite compensation by AOX-supported respiration and relatively preserved photosynthetic light reactions, mutants showed reduced CO2 fixation efficiency and severe growth defects. 3. Transcriptomics and metabolomics demonstrated broad disruption of carbon and especially nitrogen metabolism, with general amino acid depletion and reduced glycolytic/TCA intermediates. 4. External amino acid supply, but not sugars, partially rescued growth, supporting a causal role of respiration in supplying carbon skeletons for amino acid biosynthesis.
